title: Centuri Posted Record Quarterly Revenue, So Why Did One Fund Exit a $29 Million Stake?
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: ACK Asset Management fully exited its $29.11 million stake in Centuri Holdings (1,375,000 shares) in Q4 2025, despite the company posting record quarterly revenue of $850 million (up 18.1% YoY) and achieving a record $5.9 billion backlog. The exit raises questions about valuation concerns despite strong operational metrics and 45.9% share price appreciation over the past year.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Mixed signals: strong operational performance (record revenue, 25% base revenue growth, record $5.9B backlog, 45.9% YoY share appreciation) contrasts with a major fund's complete exit, suggesting valuation concerns may outweigh growth visibility despite durable demand from utility modernization trends.

Mentioned as a top holding in ACK Asset Management's portfolio ($59.03M, 7.5% of AUM), indicating it remains a core industrial/materials position, but no specific news or sentiment drivers are provided in the article.
Referenced as part of ACK's concentrated industrial/materials portfolio strategy alongside Centuri, but no specific performance or sentiment information is provided.
Mentioned as a comparable infrastructure investment in the portfolio context, but no specific news or sentiment drivers are discussed in the article.

title: What Might Be Behind One Fund's New $40 Million Bet on Rogers Corporation Stock?
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: ACK Asset Management LLC acquired 436,707 shares of Rogers Corporation for approximately $40 million in February 2026, making it a 5% position in the fund. Rogers, a supplier of engineered materials for EVs and wireless infrastructure, has gained 25% over the past year. The investment reflects confidence in the company's margin durability and exposure to EV and aerospace recovery, though the company currently trades below historical peak margins.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Strong recent performance with 25% gain over the past year, improving Q3 financials (6.5% sequential sales growth, margin expansion to 33.5%, adjusted EPS jump to $0.90), positive cash flow generation, and a significant $40M institutional investment signal confidence in future margin durability and exposure to growing EV and aerospace markets.

Mentioned as a top holding in ACK Asset Management's portfolio ($50.84M, 6.4% of AUM) alongside other industrial/infrastructure names, but no specific news or analysis provided about the company itself.
Listed as the fund's largest holding ($59.03M, 7.5% of AUM) in the same industrial/infrastructure sector, but no specific company-related news or analysis provided.
Mentioned as a comparable industrial/infrastructure holding in the fund's portfolio ($57.67M, 7.3% of AUM), but no specific news or analysis provided.

title: Novanta Stock Is Roughly Flat This Past Year, So Why Did One Investor Just Bet $45 Million on Shares?
author: Jonathan Ponciano
description: ACK Asset Management acquired 375,000 shares of Novanta worth $44.62 million in February 2026, making it a 5.6% position in the fund. Despite the stock being flat over the past year and underperforming the S&P 500, the investment suggests confidence in potential recovery as medical and advanced industrial demand rebounds. Novanta generated nearly $1 billion in trailing revenue with improving margins.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: A significant $45M institutional investment by ACK Asset Management suggests confidence in the company's recovery potential. Despite flat year-over-year performance, the company maintains strong fundamentals with ~$1B in revenue, improving EBITDA margins (23.4%), and positive guidance. The investment thesis centers on organic growth reacceleration as end markets recover, indicating the stock may be undervalued relative to its prospects.

A significant $45M institutional investment by ACK Asset Management suggests confidence in the company's recovery potential. title: US Stormwater Management Market Size Worth USD 15.05 Billion by 2033 | SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The U.S. Stormwater Management Market is projected to grow from $8.25 billion in 2025 to $15.05 billion by 2033, driven by environmental regulations, infrastructure upgrades, urbanization, and increasing adoption of green stormwater solutions.

title: Stormwater Detention System Market Size to Hit USD 876.02 Million by 2033 | Research by SNS Insider
author: Sns Insider
description: The global stormwater detention system market is projected to grow from $592.92 million in 2025 to $876.02 million by 2033, driven by urbanization, flood prevention needs, and sustainable drainage solutions.
